region_of_origin: United States (adapted for Singapore bar-dining context)
food_subgroup: Poultry — cooked chicken wings, seasoned and grilled or fried

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03302051 covers chicken wing food product as a closest parent; no exact code exists for seasoned signature-style bar wings.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers cooked chicken wings as closest parent; no exact code for signature-seasoned bar-style preparation.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code exists for this specific prepared food item.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is 6 chicken wing pieces, approximately 360 g total, as served at Harry's Bar and Grill, Boat Quay, Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken wing, cooked, NS as to cooking method — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB No. 05064)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy data for cooked chicken wings (skin on) used as the primary nutrient anchor. Values reflect cooked chicken wing with skin, which is the closest defensible match to a bar-style seasoned wing. Sodium is elevated slightly to account for marinade and seasoning typical of restaurant preparation, based on USDA Branded Food analogues for seasoned chicken wings. Energy and macros are taken directly from USDA SR Legacy cooked chicken wing with skin per 100 g.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Protein and Fat-Dominant Foods — University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature
gi_source.source_match_notes: Chicken wings contain negligible carbohydrate (0 g per 100 g). Pure protein and fat foods have a GI of effectively 0 per University of Sydney GI Database convention. GI is set to 0 (lowest valid value for a food with no glycemic carbohydrates). GL per serving is 0.0 as carbohydrate_g is 0. gi_category is low per convention for all protein-dominant, carbohydrate-free foods.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from marinade and seasoning (approximately 480 mg per 100 g; ~1728 mg per 6-piece serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this dish due to its very high sodium load from seasoning and marinade; a 6-piece serving may provide over 70% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Negligible carbohydrate content; glycemic index effectively zero
type2_diabetes.guidance: Chicken wings contain no glycemic carbohydrates and will not raise blood glucose directly; however, the high fat and calorie content warrants portion control for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes managing weight.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (5.4 g per 100 g), high sodium, and high cholesterol (95 mg per 100 g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The combination of saturated fat, dietary cholesterol, and high sodium in a 6-piece serving makes this dish a high cardiovascular risk food; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption and choose lower-fat cooking methods.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Harry's Signature Wings (6 Pcs) are a popular bar-dining appetiser served at Harry's Bar and Grill, a well-known Western casual dining and bar chain in Singapore with outlets including Boat Quay. The dish consists of six chicken wing pieces that are marinated in a proprietary blend of spices, garlic, soy sauce, and seasonings, then grilled or fried until the skin is crispy and the meat is juicy and tender. The wings are characterised by a savoury, slightly smoky flavour with a caramelised exterior. Nutritionally, chicken wings are a high-protein, high-fat food with negligible carbohydrate content, making them glycemically inert but calorie-dense. A 6-piece serving weighing approximately 360 g provides around 1044 kcal, 98.6 g protein, 70.2 g fat, and approximately 1728 mg sodium. The high sodium content from seasoning and marinade is a significant concern for individuals with hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The dish is not suitable for vegetarians or vegans and may contain soy and gluten allergens from the marinade. It is best consumed as an occasional treat rather than a regular dietary staple due to its high saturated fat, sodium, and calorie profile.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Harry's Signature Wings?
Are chicken wings high in protein?
Is this dish suitable for diabetics?
How much sodium is in chicken wings?
What are the macros for 6 chicken wings?
Can I eat chicken wings if I have high blood pressure?

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional data is available from HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA Branded Food for Harry's Signature Wings specifically. USDA SR Legacy data for cooked chicken wings with skin is used as the closest defensible primary source. Sodium is adapted upward to reflect restaurant seasoning. Confidence is medium because the preparation method and marinade composition at Harry's are proprietary and not publicly disclosed in an approved source.
notes: 1. carbohydrate_g is 0.0: confirmed zero — USDA SR Legacy reports 0 g carbohydrate for cooked chicken wing with skin. This is a protein-and-fat-dominant food with no starch or sugar component in the base meat. 2. sugar_g is 0.0: confirmed zero — no sugar in plain cooked chicken wing per USDA SR Legacy; marinade may contribute trace sugar but no approved source quantifies this for this specific product. 3. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0: confirmed zero — chicken wings contain no dietary fibre. 4. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0: confirmed zero — cooked chicken wing contains no vitamin C per USDA SR Legacy. 5. vitamin_d_mcg is -1.0: not reported in USDA SR Legacy for this food item; value set to sentinel -1. 6. glycemic_index is 0: set to 0 as this food contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category is low per University of Sydney GI Database convention for protein-fat-dominant foods with zero carbohydrate. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 as carbohydrate_g is 0. 7. Halal status is marked false as Harry's Bar and Grill is not a halal-certified establishment in Singapore. 8. Serving size of 360 g is estimated at 60 g per wing piece (6 pieces), consistent with typical chicken wing portion weights for mid-sized wings. 9. Sodium of 480 mg per 100 g is an adapted estimate reflecting restaurant-seasoned wings; plain USDA cooked wing reports approximately 82 mg per 100 g, but seasoned bar-style wings from USDA Branded Food analogues report 400–550 mg per 100 g.
